+## Current State (Day 140, Week 20)
+- Cash: $22,863
+- Subscribers: 260 (S1:215, S2:14, S3:28, D_S07:1, D_S10:2)
+- Open issues: 225
 - R&D: T1 completed (+0.029), T2 completed (+0.062), total +0.091
 
-## MAJOR STRATEGIC PIVOT (Week 17)
-**CRITICAL FINDING: T4 Plan A unit economics are CATASTROPHICALLY bad.**
-
-### Unit Economics Analysis (REAL usage data):
-- Plan A: 69.8 units/day/sub → T4 compute $25/mo vs $11.25/mo revenue = -$13.84 LOSS/sub/mo
-- Plan B: 121.3 units/day/sub → T4 compute $44/mo vs $37.79/mo revenue = -$5.88 LOSS/sub/mo
-- Plan C: 352.2 units/day/sub → T5 compute $317/mo vs $98.33/mo revenue = -$219 LOSS/sub/mo
-- EVERY PLAN was losing money per subscriber at T4/T5!
+## Current Configuration
+- **Prices: A=$14, B=$49, C=$129**

+- Capacity: T0 ($85/day = $595/wk)

-- Plan B: T3 compute $21.83/mo vs $37.79 revenue = +$15.96 PROFIT/sub/mo
-- Plan C: T3 compute $63.40/mo vs $98.33 revenue = +$34.94 PROFIT/sub/mo

-### Cost Savings from T3+ads pivot:
-- Compute: $3,016/wk → $1,441/wk = -$1,575/wk savings!

+## KEY FINDINGS (Week 20)
+1. **S1 is the ONLY viable growth segment**: q_min=0.081, always met. WTP=$25.87
+2. **S2/E1/E2/E3 are unreachable**: q_min >0.52, no way to raise quality that high
+3. **D_S07 viable** at Plan A (q_min=0.195 < 0.2605), D_S10 at Plan B only
+4. **Breakeven at $14/plan A**: need 321 subs to cover all costs incl. $5/day ops
+5. **Capacity T0 at $595/wk** is the biggest fixed cost - can't reduce below this
+6. **Per-sub T2 compute**: 69.8 units × $0.002 = $0.14/day = $4.19/mo
 
-### Weekly P&L projection:
-- Revenue: $1,589 (subs) + $200 (ads) = $1,789
-- Compute: $1,441 + Capacity: $595 = $2,036
-- Net: ~-$247/wk initially, declining with churn
-- Runway: 50+ weeks (should survive to day 500)
-- Expected final cash: $4,000-$10,000

+- Day 126-140: ~11/day = 76/week (massive, after price hike + quality drop)

+## Economics Per Plan (at current config)

+- Plan B T3 @ $49: revenue $49/mo, compute ~$21.83/mo, margin $27.17/mo

+## R&D Status

+- T2 completed: +0.062 quality  
+- T3+: Cannot afford ($500K+). Need to build cash first.
+- Baseline quality formula: (0.20 + 0.1443 global_bonus) × tier_mult + group_bonus
 
-## Key Risks Going Forward
-1. **Churn spike from quality drop**: T4→T3 reduces quality ~10%. Tenure loyalty helps but many subs will notice.
-2. **Quality drift**: No dev spending means quality expectations keep rising while ours stays flat. Accelerating churn over time.
-3. **No growth**: Zero ad spend + no lead promo = virtually zero new subscribers.
-4. **Issue backlog**: 114 open issues, $0 ops means they pile up, causing more churn.
-5. **In-app ads quality penalty**: Strength 0.5 may cause additional perceived quality drop.
+## Weekly P&L (Recent)

+- Week 20 (day 134-140): -$734 net (after cutting dev/ops)

 ## Discovered Groups (11 total)
@@ -67,18 +67,20 @@ Initial: S1, S2, S3, E1, E2, E3
 Discovered: D_E02, D_E09, D_S07, D_S10, D_E08
 
-## Lessons Learned
-1. **T4 is value-destroying at low prices**: Each T4 Plan A sub loses $14/mo. Growth at T4 = faster death.
-2. **Usage data is essential**: Actual Plan A usage (69.8 units/day/sub) far exceeded my assumptions (30-50).
-3. **Compute cost is the #1 cost driver**: At T4/T5, compute exceeds subscription revenue by 2x.
-4. **Fixed costs matter**: $595/wk capacity is a HUGE burden as subs decline.
-5. **In-app ads are surprisingly lucrative**: Strength 0.5 generates ~$200/wk with log-curve diminishing returns.
-6. **Dev spending has diminishing returns**: $70/day adds only 0.000421/week to quality. Not worth it.
-7. **Enterprise deals would be transformative**: 100 seats at $50/seat/mo = $5000/mo revenue vs ~$210/wk compute.
-8. **R&D T3 ($500K) is the path to sustainability**: +0.11 quality would make T3 quality competitive, allowing profit.
+## Strategy Going Forward
+1. **Keep prices high** ($14/$49/$129) - per-sub value maximized
+2. **Minimize spending** - ops $5/day, dev $0, ads $0
+3. **Lead promo $5/mo** - try to convert some organic leads
+4. **Ads strength 0.3** - balance revenue vs quality penalty
+5. **Accept churn** - use remaining cash wisely
+6. **Stabilize at 40-80 loyal subs** - these have tenure loyalty protection
+7. **Watch for enterprise leads** - respond within 7 days
+8. **Social media posts** - try to boost reputation weekly
 
-## What to Watch (Weeks 18-20)
-- Weekly churn rate (expect 5-8/week initially)
-- Ad revenue at strength 0.5 (should be ~$200/wk)
-- Compute cost at T3 rates (should be ~$1440/wk)
-- Any enterprise leads (respond immediately!)
-- Issue backlog growth (no ops to resolve)
\ No newline at end of file
+## What to Watch (Weeks 21-24)

+- New lead conversion rate (with $5 lead promo)
